methodology: Derived from customer KPI pyramids, strategy horizons, and delivery activity using an OKR-inspired structure.

goals.title: 2026-Q2 Prove that city intelligence drives real policy decisions
goals.objective: Turn Arrive's city-planning proposition into a credible expansion path by linking operational mobility signals with intervention and outcome modeling.

linkedCustomers.customerGroup: City Authorities and Urban Mobility Planners

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Time needed to move from intervention idea to outcome-backed decision.
Time drivers spend searching for parking before finding a valid place to stop.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Recurring revenue from city and authority accounts using planning, parking, and mobility solutions.

keyResults.target: Help cities move faster from question to evidence-backed action
keyResults.baseline: Policy decisions still rely on fragmented evidence and manual analysis
keyResults.statement: Reduce policy experiment decision cycle by providing reusable planning scenarios tied to operational data.

keyResults.target: Use better parking and curb policy to reduce search traffic
keyResults.baseline: Cities still struggle to tie interventions to visible search-time outcomes
keyResults.statement: Reduce average parking search time by enabling faster pricing and curb-policy iteration based on shared city metrics.

keyResults.baseline: Expansion depends on making planning and operations feel like one coherent offer
keyResults.statement: Increase public-sector platform ARR by proving the value of city intelligence as an attach and expansion driver.

linkedInitiatives.description: City intelligence operating model initiative focused on improving city mobility intelligence, planning, scenario & outcome modeling, operational data, KPI & analytics platform, and enterprise integration layer across City Mobility Insight Board and Mobility Intelligence Data API.

goals.title: 2026-Q2 Turn business mobility into a cleaner admin and billing product
goals.objective: Prepare the next quarter around stronger fleet and workplace parking control so business accounts see lower admin burden and better spend governance.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Number of employee reimbursement artifacts requiring manual handling.
Share of parking usage that falls outside defined business policy.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Recurring revenue from fleets, employers, and business mobility accounts.

keyResults.target: Remove manual reimbursement work from everyday parking use
keyResults.baseline: Admin teams still handle too many ad hoc parking artifacts
keyResults.statement: Reduce receipts per employee by moving more usage into centralized policy, billing, and tagging flows.

keyResults.target: Tighten business parking governance without adding friction
keyResults.baseline: Policy drift is still common across locations and temporary access cases
keyResults.statement: Reduce policy exception rate by introducing clearer entitlements, visitor workflows, and account controls.

keyResults.target: Make business mobility a more expandable product line
keyResults.baseline: Expansion depends on cleaner admin economics and better cost control
keyResults.statement: Increase business and fleet ARR by proving that Arrive reduces admin overhead and improves spend visibility.

description: Preparation goals for the next quarter.
